svg.title: SDD applied to the general Software Development Life Cycle
svg.desc: A diagram showing the standard SDLC phases (Requirements, Design, Development, Testing, Deployment, Maintenance) with SDD activities injected at each phase.

g.onclick: sendPrompt('How does SDD change requirements gathering?')
sendPrompt('How to write structured specs from requirements?')
sendPrompt('How does SDD affect system design?')
sendPrompt('How do specs become the source of truth during design?')
g.text: — not a wiki or doc tool

g.onclick: sendPrompt('How does SDD change the development phase?')
sendPrompt('How does spec-first AI coding work in practice?')
sendPrompt('What is the NEEDS CLARIFICATION marker pattern?')
sendPrompt('How do specs generate test cases automatically?')
sendPrompt('What is a spec compliance gate before deployment?')
g.text: PR must cite or update the spec

g.onclick: sendPrompt('How does SDD help with maintenance?')

svg.text: Spec-first — write spec, guide AI, spec may be discarded after
Guardrail — AI stops on ambiguity instead of guessing
Spec-anchored — spec stays alive as source of truth
Start with spec-first on 2-3 features. Graduate to spec-anchored
for high-change areas. The spec is iterative — write enough for
the next cycle, not the entire feature upfront.
Click any box to explore that phase in detail.
